reviews.text:
I had a long and challenging journey with my workers’ compensation case, and choosing Jason Weinstock Law was the best decision I made. Before visiting his office, I thoroughly reviewed his website, which stood out as far more informative and helpful than other firms’. What mattered most to me was finding a way to change doctors, and within a month, I was seeing a top surgeon in Las Vegas. The previous workers’ comp doctor had been treating me for a pulled muscle, but an MRI ordered by the surgeon revealed a far more serious injury—something that would have gone unnoticed had I stayed with the original doctor.
Jason also stepped in to resolve my medication issues, ensuring they arrived on schedule rather than consistently late. His proactive approach made a huge difference in my recovery process.
Throughout my case, I shared my experience with others, and many wished they had gotten a lawyer. Their stories reinforced how grateful I was for having Jason. My case lasted three years, and giving Jason a five-star rating feels like awarding a trusted friend—it’s the highest I can give, but he deserves even more.
I highly recommend Weinstock Law. Jason genuinely cares, and his legal assistant, Anna, is exceptional. They make an outstanding team, and even now, after my case has been closed and their fees settled, Jason and Anna still check in to see how I’m doing. Their dedication goes beyond the legal work—they truly care. When you meet them, you’ll understand why I consider them more than just a law firm

reviews.text:
I gave this law firm 2 stars because my time is valuable. It  was a waste of my time going to their office. Nora Trujilio documented specific details pertaining to my case, and the incident a week ago. She was aware that my injury happened in another state not Nevada. I inquired about if  the attorneys at this firm would have jurisdiction to take on my case she said yes.

I arrived today, and no attorneys for this office were there. I understand the personal matters  happen. However, another attorney should have been able to fill in when circumstances arise.  A scheduled consultation should be the attorney or attorneys' not the intake specialist. This just didn't sit well with me as a ethical law firm.

The issue is not that this firm is not willing or cannot  take my case. It is  always best to always keep options open when considering representation  for a lawsuit in general.

My issue is the inconvenience that this has caused me.  Nora did contact an attorney over the phone when I was there. I spoke with her. She explained that she could not take on the case because it happened in a different state. Nora expressed that where the incident occurred "would not" be an issue a week ago.if she was unsure, she  should not have informed me about it.

I sat in their office and  discussed my case for over 20 minutes at the office in a conference room with Nora before the attorney got on a phone call   and spoke to me on speaker. I was treated very poorly, besides receiving a water bottle from the nice receptionist when I first entered the building. She made me feel welcomed. Nora seemed friendly as well don't get me wrong.

She just mislead me. She  misinformed me about the attorney Jeff and the other attorney being able to take on my case. I am just disappointed by these individuals. Their lack on integrity is unacceptable. If they were not able to take on my case this should  have been addressed during the intake a week ago.

Instead, it was addressed when I took a trip to their office  way on the other side of town. I thought that I was moving forward, but I am starting over at step one again. I also disliked  that the attorney on the phone gave me no hope after my time was wasted. She drew the conclusion  stating that no other law firm in Nevada could take on my case.

Some attorneys'  have the ability to be practice on multi -jurisdiction level. I will contact different offices, and the Nevada State Bar and find out for myself.  It may still be possible to find representation in the state of Nevada. I will research attorneys in the state where the incident occurred as well. My journey continues.

How people treat me  will always be rememberable to me  no matter if it is a positive or a negative experience. My experience at Temple Injury Law Firm was  not a positive experience for me. I would not recommend this law firm to anyone after this negative experience.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We're very sorry for the inconvenience you experienced and understand how frustrating it must have been to arrive for an appointment and not have an attorney there in person.

Unfortunately, hours before your scheduled consultation, my husband and law partner underwent emergency eye surgery due to a sudden retinal detachment. It was an extremely serious and frightening situation, and our priority in that moment was preserving his vision.

As a small, two-attorney family firm, an emergency like this has an immediate and unavoidable impact. We did our best to still see you so that you didn’t have to reschedule appointments. I even called in for your appointment from the rehabilitation center.

During our conversation, we also tried to help you understand that your slip-and-fall case—having occurred in Ohio—would need to be handled by an attorney licensed in Ohio. As Nevada attorneys, we are legally prohibited from handling cases outside our jurisdiction. Despite this, we offered to go out of our way to help you find someone licensed in both states, which is quite rare.

It seemed that the frustration and anger you had with us last week was more about the refusal to take a case we legally could not accept. We truly wish you had waited to leave this review until after speaking with other firms—because you would have seen a common theme: any ethical Nevada attorney honoring the law and acting with integrity would have told you the same thing.

We believe in treating everyone with kindness and respect and always go above and beyond to help where we can. It’s unfortunate that this experience didn’t meet your expectations. We do wish, especially in difficult times like these, that compassion could go both ways. Emergencies happen, and a little understanding can go a long way.

We wish you all the best in resolving your legal matter and hope you find the right attorney for your case.

Marissa Temple
Attorney at Temple Injury Law

reviews.text: I had David Jacks as co-counsel on a child custody case. We attended a hearing together on a motion involving make-up visitation. While we waited outside the court to be called in, I gave him a rundown of the facts, issues, and the legal arguments I intended to present. Without skipping a beat, David asked why we were not using a specific rule and he cited the exact code reference to the rule. I pulled out my iPhone and looked up the rule and sure enough he was right. We then went into the court and I presented the arguments and the judge looked at me quizzically and said that she had never heard of that rule before in all her years on the bench. She pulled out her rulebook and looked it up and sure enough David was right and we won on that issue thanks to his memory and knowledge of the law and his ability to quickly analyze facts.

reviews.text: My husband left me on Halloween, which was 37 years after our first date.  I spent the year before he left nursing him through cancer and I was floored.  I had no plans, no money saved, no job, and I was responsible for our son, who has autism.  When he left, I was in my final year of the degree I had struggled to finish for most of our marriage.  He told me that he would only pay me alimony for two years.  I was terrified.  How would I pay for health insurance?  I would lose our house, and I would probably work until I died.  My sister’s husband,  a trial attorney, recommended David Jacks.  After our first meeting I was so relieved.  I found out that I was due alimony for at least half the length of our marriage (or longer).  I also found out my husband would be responsible for a lot more than he tried to tell me!  David was professional and supportive throughout the process.  My husband and I negotiated for almost a year, and David was patient with all of the changes we made.  Eventually, David helped me get a settlement that gave me enough alimony to finish school and pay off a house.  In addition, I found out I was due half my husband’s retirement, so I wouldn’t be penniless in my old age!  My life is so much better than when I was married.  I have peace of mind and an exciting future ahead.  Everything changed the day I walked into David’s office.  I am so grateful I found him. I cant say enough about the excellent representation and legal services my wife and I received from Cardenas Law!

We had an accident (we were not at fault) but due to the circumstances of the accident, we were unsure if we had any legal recourse to pursue compensation against the driver of the other vehicle.

For a few hours after the accident, I even considered fixing my vehicle on my own and just calling it good.

On a whim, I gave Cardenas Law a call and was relieved to find out that we had a legitimate claim for compensation and they would take care of it.

They absolutely did.

When we started encountering musculo-skeletal issues immediately after the accident (our insurer fixed our vehicle but could have cared less about our injuries), Cardenas Law set us up with some of the BEST rehabilitation services I have ever experienced.

The rehabilitation services were excellent and we actually got better!

Cardenas Law kept us updated with concise and timely updates and was always available when we had questions and concerns.

Accidents suck but our experience with Cardenas Law really helped us navigate a terrible situation. We weren't expecting much other than repairs and rehabilitation but Cardenas Law even got ample compensation for lost work time and suffering.

Again, accidents suck but with a partner like Cardenas Law representing us, they took a whole barrel of lemons and turned it into lemonade.

For the life of me, I pray I never am involved in another accident BUT if I am, Cardenas Law is my first call!

Thank you again Rob and your staff for turning this nightmare into the best experience it could be and helping us get our lives back!

reviews.ownerResponse: We appreciate everyone who contacts our office and reaches out to Gabroy | Messer, our AV-Preeminent law firm, to possibly assist them,  support them, and help them. In the civil rights context, our cases are very difficult to pursue and get justice against our clients against very secured and well-heeled wrongdoers. We fight hard for our clients we are able to accept. Our intake form states that our firm may contact you by your preferred contact method for a possible consult, to request further necessary information, to advise we may not be able to assist you, or that a referral may be necessary. Because of the complex nature of our area of law, the limited selection that our firm chooses for initial consultation, and the amount of time required to complete due diligence we are not able to take on every matter. We are very proud of our justice results, very proud of our clients for standing up to corporate indifference and desire that the laws be better to protect and preserve our client's interests and make sure such claims are pursued to the fullest extent of the law by all lawful and ethical measures. We also will never take any frivolous claims and must be selective to protect our community.  It is an honor for Gabroy | Messer to represent our clients with zealous advocacy to the fullest extent of the law.  Although our office desires to fully respond to this review, our professional obligations limit our office in responding to reviews.

Our experience with Cohan PLLC and Attorney Chasen Cohan has been outstanding from start to finish. After my mother was injured in a serious incident in Las Vegas, I reached out to Chasen personally and he took the time to hear every detail of the accident. From the very beginning, he made sure we felt supported and informed. There was hurdles we thought we needed to figure out, but Chasen assured that though he was based in Las Vegas that he has experience with working with other clients in other states such as California.

What truly set Cohan PLLC apart was how hands-on and organized their team was with coordinating medical care. There were changes that occurred on his team; nonetheless, he kept me in the loop with the different staff changes. The team helped schedule and manage a wide range of appointments, including emergency care, physical therapy, orthopedic specialists, pain management, telemedicine visits, and radiology services. With their coordination, my mom was able to continue treatment without delays or financial stress, with medical bills placed on lien and out-of-pocket expenses addressed whenever possible.

The communication through text threads that included Chasen and his team, which made it incredibly easy to share updates, ask questions, send photos, and handle paperwork like HIPAA forms and medical records in real time. Anytime something came up such as an appointment issues, referrals, or billing questions - the team was quick to respond and resolve it.

Throughout the entire process, Chasen and his staff were patient, compassionate, and genuinely invested in my mom’s recovery. They checked in, explained next steps, and advocated for her care every step of the way.

I highly recommend Cohan PLLC to anyone looking for a personal injury firm that is organized, responsive, and truly cares about their clients. Chasen Cohan and his team went above and beyond for our family, and we are incredibly thankful for their support.

Chasen Cohan is an excellent attorney! I was in a car accident that totaled my car. I had injuries and needed medical care. I was happy with my lawyer for almost two years and truly felt he was on my side. However, towards the end of my case—despite having a very strong claim—he seemed to back away and strongly urged me to accept a very small settlement. He pressured me into agreeing by emphasizing how expensive trial would be.

What upset me the most was the way the settlement was structured. My lawyer ended up walking away with more money than I received, and I was left paying an unreasonable amount of taxes on money that should never have been categorized that way. I voiced my concern, but was told “this is usual.” Half of my settlement was labeled as lost wages, even though the opposing lawyers had argued my wage loss was only about 10%.

In the end, I felt pressured, misled, and left with far less than what was fair. I regret trusting how the final stage of my case was handled.

Feedback on My Experience

Dear HKM,

Thank you for your recent response regarding my case. While I appreciate the communication and respect your decision, I must express my disappointment with the overall experience.

After numerous attempts to contact your firm over the past two weeks, I was surprised to receive a response only after you decided not to take my case. This lack of proactive communication left me feeling sidelined and unvalued as a potential client. Additionally, it was concerning to see references to conversations that never occurred, which further diminished my trust in the process.

I also appreciate the references you provided; however, it is troubling that one of those references was mentioned in my initial inquiry. This indicates that my inquiry may not have been thoroughly read, as it contained important context that should have informed your assessment.

I believe that client care includes timely and transparent communication, especially when dealing with sensitive matters like employment issues. Unfortunately, my experience did not reflect this expectation, leading me to rate my experience as one star.

I hope this feedback can help improve the client experience for others in the future.

Thank you for your time.

Sincerely,
Past Client
